title: Tumor Growth Model Inversion
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Tumor Growth Model Inversion: Tumor growth inversion: estimate tumor growth rate lambda and carrying capacity K from longitudinal imaging data. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.ode.gompertz_logistic…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~20; imaging_noise_percent, resistant_subclone_emergence set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: Gompertz: dV/dt = lambda * V * ln(K/V); Logistic: dV/dt = r*V*(1-V/K); Exponential-Linear for early growth
E.world_state_x: tumor growth parameters (lambda/r, K, V0) and treatment effect delta_kill
E.observation_y: tumor volume V(t_i) from CT/MRI imaging at N_timepoints

W.regime: Existence of the recovered tumor_growth_parameter_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 20); imaging_noise_percent dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Lognormal sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: volume prediction within 20% for calibrated model. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~20. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |imaging_noise_percent|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_timepoints * N_params) per ODE fit per iteration
